mission.title: Install and Configure kflashback (Kubernetes Resource History)
mission.description: kflashback is a Kubernetes operator that tracks resource history — every create, update, and delete — with revision diffs, AI-powered insights, and anomaly detection. It records a complete audit trail of your cluster's state over time with configurable retention policies. Supports local LLMs (Ollama) or cloud providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) for natural-language queries like "What changed in the last hour?" and automatic anomaly detection.

steps.title: Install kflashback CRDs
steps.description:
Apply the Custom Resource Definitions that kflashback uses to store configuration and tracking policies.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/crd/flashback.io_kflashbackconfigs.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/crd/flashback.io_flashbackpolicies.yaml

```

> Installs the KFlashbackConfig and FlashbackPolicy CRDs into your cluster.

steps.title: Create the kflashback namespace and RBAC
steps.description:
Set up the namespace, service account, and RBAC rules that the kflashback controller needs.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kflashback-system --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/rbac/service_account.yaml -n kflashback-system

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/rbac/role.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/rbac/role_binding.yaml

```

> Creates the namespace and grants the controller permission to watch cluster resources.
commands: kubectl create namespace kflashback-system --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

steps.title: Deploy the kflashback controller
steps.description:
Deploy the kflashback operator. The controller watches for FlashbackPolicy resources and begins tracking the specified Kubernetes resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/manager/deployment.yaml -n kflashback-system

```

> Deploys the kflashback controller from the latest container image.

steps.title: Create a KFlashbackConfig
steps.description:
Configure the kflashback instance with SQLite storage and default server ports.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: flashback.io/v1alpha1
kind: KFlashbackConfig
metadata:
  name: kflashback
  namespace: kflashback-system
spec:
  storage:
    backend: sqlite
    dsn: /data/kflashback.db
  server:
    apiAddress: ":9090"
    healthAddress: ":8081"
  controller:
    leaderElection: true
    reconcileInterval: "5m"
EOF

```

> Configures kflashback with SQLite (lightweight, no external DB needed).
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: flashback.io/v1alpha1
kind: KFlashbackConfig
metadata:
  name: kflashback
  namespace: kflashback-system
spec:
  storage:
    backend: sqlite
    dsn: /data/kflashback.db
  server:
    apiAddress: ":9090"
    healthAddress: ":8081"
  controller:
    leaderElection: true
    reconcileInterval: "5m"
EOF

steps.title: Create a FlashbackPolicy to track workloads
steps.description:
Define which Kubernetes resources kflashback should track. This sample policy tracks Deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets, and Services.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: flashback.io/v1alpha1
kind: FlashbackPolicy
metadata:
  name: track-workloads
  namespace: kflashback-system
spec:
  resources:
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
        - kube-public
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: StatefulSet
      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: DaemonSet
      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
    - apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
        - kube-public
  retention:
    maxAge: "720h"
    maxRevisions: 500
  storage:
    snapshotEvery: 20
    compressSnapshots: true
  tracking:
    creations: true
    updates: true
    deletions: true
  paused: false
EOF

```

> Tracks all workload changes (create/update/delete) with 30-day retention.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: flashback.io/v1alpha1
kind: FlashbackPolicy
metadata:
  name: track-workloads
  namespace: kflashback-system
spec:
  resources:
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
        - kube-public
    - apiVersion: apps/v1

      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: DaemonSet
      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
    - apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      excludeNamespaces:
        - kube-system
        - kube-public

  storage:
    snapshotEvery: 20
    compressSnapshots: true
  tracking:
    creations: true
    updates: true
    deletions: true
  paused: false
EOF

steps.title: Verify kflashback is running
steps.description:
Check that the controller pod is running and the CRDs are registered.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kflashback-system

kubectl get kflashbackconfigs -n kflashback-system

kubectl get flashbackpolicies -n kflashback-system

```

> You should see the controller pod in Running state and both custom resources listed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall kflashback
uninstall.description:
Remove kflashback from your cluster. This deletes all tracked history data.

```bash

kubectl delete flashbackpolicies --all -n kflashback-system

kubectl delete kflashbackconfigs --all -n kflashback-system

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/manager/deployment.yaml -n kflashback-system

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/rbac/role_binding.yaml

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/rbac/role.yaml

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/rbac/service_account.yaml -n kflashback-system

kubectl delete namespace kflashback-system

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/crd/flashback.io_flashbackpolicies.yaml

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prashanthjos/kflashback/main/config/crd/flashback.io_kflashbackconfigs.yaml

```

> Removes all kflashback resources, RBAC, CRDs, and namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Controller pod is in ImagePullBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The GHCR container image may not be publicly accessible (known issue #3 on the kflashback repo).

**Fix:**
Build and push the image locally:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/prashanthjos/kflashback.git
cd kflashback
make docker-build IMG=kflashback:local
kind load docker-image kflashback:local  # if using kind
```
Then update the deployment to use `kflashback:local` with `imagePullPolicy: Never`.

troubleshooting.title: CRD validation errors on apply
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes version may be too old for the CRD schema.

**Fix:**
kflashback requires Kubernetes 1.25+. Check your version with `kubectl version` and upgrade if needed.

troubleshooting.title: No history being recorded
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The FlashbackPolicy may not match any resources, or the controller may not have RBAC to watch them.

**Fix:**
Check controller logs: `kubectl logs -n kflashback-system -l app=kflashback`. Verify the policy's resource kinds and namespace filters match your workloads.
